changes.old:
Flash, Flying
When The Fiend enters the battlefield, starting with you and proceeding in turn order, each player may bargain. Each player who doesn't bargain for the first time this way discards a card, loses 5 life, and mills three cards.
Then, starting with you and proceeding in turn order, each player may bargain again. Repeat this process until all players decline.

• Power — Win: Put six +1/+1 counters on each creature you control. Lose: Sacrifice six creatures.
• Knowledge — Win: Draw six cards. Lose: Mill 6 cards.
• Wealth — Win: Search your library for six land cards, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle your library. Lose: Sacrifice six lands.
• Vengeance — Win: Target player sacrifices six permanents. Lose: You sacrifice six permanents.
• Immortality — Win: You gain 66 life. Lose: Your life total is reduced to 6.
• Dominion — Win: Gain control of six target permanents target player controls. Lose: Target opponent gains control of six permanents you control.
Whenever The Fiend becomes the target of a spell or ability, is dealt damage, or if all players refused to bargain this turn, shuffle it into its owner's library.

The Map.message: Errata pass: 50+ card fixes across all four decks

changes.old:
When The Map enters the battlefield, put three seek counters on it.
{T}, Remove a seek counter from The Map: Choose one —
• Locate — Name a card. Reveal cards from the top of target player's library until you reveal the named card. Put the named card from among them into your hand, shuffle the rest into that player's library. That player loses life equal to that card's mana value.
• Plunder — Look at target player's hand and choose a nonland card from it. That player discards that card. You may cast that card this turn and spend mana as though it were mana of any color to cast it. If you don't cast it this turn, exile it.
• Manifest — Search your library for an artifact, enchantment, or planeswalker card with mana value 4 or less and put it onto the battlefield. If it's a planeswalker, it enters with an additional loyalty counter on it. Then shuffle.

The Crossroads.message: Errata pass: 50+ card fixes across all four decks

changes.old:
Roll a twenty-sided die.
If the result is even — Each player's life total becomes equal to rolling a one-hundred-sided die.
If the result is odd — Target opponent and you exchange life totals, then roll two d10s. You gain life equal to the first roll and each opponent loses life equal to the second roll. This life loss can't reduce a player's life total below 1.
changes.new:
Blink Step — Roll a d12 and put that many blink counters on you.
As long as you have a blink counter, at the beginning of combat on your turn, you may remove a blink counter. If you do, exile target creature you control, then return it to the battlefield under your control. It gains haste until end of turn.

changes.old:

Shimmer — When The Fey enters, exile your hand and library. Create X 0/1 green and blue Faerie creature tokens with flying, where X is the number of cards exiled this way.
If you would draw a card while your library has no cards in it, skip that draw instead.
Whenever a Faerie you control leaves play, look at three cards at random exiled with The Fey. You may cast one of them at random this turn without paying its mana cost.

If The Fey leaves play, shuffle all cards exiled with it into their owner's library.

The Bridge.message: Errata pass: 50+ card fixes across all four decks

changes.old:
The Bridge enters the battlefield with three time counters on it.
{1}, {T}, Remove a time counter from The Bridge: End the turn. Activate this ability anytime you could cast an instant.
(Exile all spells and abilities from the stack, including this ability. The player whose turn it is discards down to their maximum hand size. Damage wears off, and "this turn" and "until end of turn" effects end.)
When you remove a time counter from The Bridge, scry 2.
When the last time counter is removed, sacrifice The Bridge and take an extra turn. During that turn, you may play an additional land and draw two cards at the beginning of your draw step.
changes.new:
The Bridge enters with a number of portal counters on it equal to 2 plus the result of rolling a d12.
{T}, Remove a portal counter: Exile target creature you control, then return it to the battlefield under your control. You may have it enter the battlefield as a copy of another creature on the battlefield until end of turn.
When The Bridge has no portal counters on it, sacrifice it.
